title: aita for refusing to give up my personal space?
text: last sunday, i (15f) was talking to my cousin about how i thought it was gross that some guy who commented on my tiktok post said i was cute. his mom walked in and heard about this and asked me about it so i pretty much was like “yeah its nothing its actually kinda funny to me” and she was like okay. not to long later my mom came back and took my aunt to the store. when she came back we were leaving and she asked me about the comment saying that my aunt told her all about it. she thought it was some old guy when in reality the guy was like 13 it wasn’t that big of a deal. she then proceeded to say “well i want your tiktok username and password.” i, being a closeted lesbian, didn’t really want her to have that password because i prefer to stay.. closeted when it comes to my family. i unwillingly agreed but deleted everything pointing to me being gay. later that night when i got back to my dads house she texted me asking for all of my instagram and snapchat passwords, even my google email? see now im a very open person when it comes to social media and ive been very careful so that they dont find out about my accounts but this is really starting to freak me out. she threatened that she was going to deactivate my accounts and its just not fair to me. she then proceeded to say that if i didnt give her what she wanted she was going to get my dad involved since i live with him. its also about the moral though. why cant i have my own personal space?? im a teenager not a child and i dont need my social media accounts monitored my by mother. i want to be able to have my own space and since i pretty much have no control over my life, my social accounts are really all i have that is fully mine. i left my mom on delivered but i dont know if what im doing is right or if i should just comply because shes my mother and im her daughter. am i the asshole?

storyboard.content: I'm 15 years old and a closeted lesbian. I prefer to keep my sexuality hidden from my family, and my social media accounts are where I express that part of myself.
I was talking to my cousin about a boy around 13 who commented on my TikTok saying I was cute. I thought it was gross but also kind of funny -- it wasn't a big deal.
My aunt overheard the conversation and told my mom about the comment. My mom thought it was some old guy when it was really just a 13-year-old kid.
My mom demanded my TikTok username and password.
I unwillingly agreed, but first deleted everything pointing to me being gay.
Later that night, my mom texted me demanding all of my Instagram, Snapchat, and even Google email passwords.
My mom threatened to deactivate my accounts and said if I didn't comply she would get my dad involved.
I left my mom on delivered and have not given her the rest of my passwords.
I'm a teenager, not a child, and I don't need my social media monitored by my mother. My social accounts are really all I have that is fully mine.
